verdict: conservative (controls false positives; p-values non-uniform in the SAFE direction)
interpretation: A symmetric (zero-directedness) DGP produces false 'directed' calls only 3.0% of the time at alpha=0.05 (target <=5%): the test does not manufacture significance from finite-sample noise. Non-uniformity of the p-values reflects the flip null's magnitude-preserving conservatism, which errs toward NOT flagging directedness.
